history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails immediately on the first step because the assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin generates assertion classes that reference package-private inner classes, triggering 'inaccessible class or interface' errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Prepend a POM_PATCH step to force the assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin to version 2.1.0, which skips non-public classes during generation and resolves the compile-time accessibility conflict before any OpenRewrite recipes are applied.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails during assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin execution because it generates assertions for package-private inner classes, causing inaccessible class errors and method signature conflicts with AssertJ core.
history.qwen_rationale: Forcing the assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin to 2.3.0 and assertj-core to 3.24.2 should resolve the generation conflicts and inaccessible class handling, as newer versions include improved filtering and compatibility with modern AssertJ signatures. This POM_PATCH step runs before any recipe application to ensure the build compiles cleanly under JDK 11.

history.qwen_observation: Plugin resolution failed for assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin:2.3.0 in the local Nexus, and prior attempts with 2.1.0 failed during code generation due to inaccessible inner classes.
history.qwen_rationale: Bumping the generator plugin to 2.5.0 (latest stable) alongside assertj-core 3.24.2 should resolve the missing artifact error and likely includes fixes for inner-class visibility that caused previous compilation failures. The remainder follows the standard JDK 11→21 migration path.

history.qwen_observation: The assertj-assertions-generator-maven-plugin version 2.5.0 is missing from the private Nexus repository, causing a plugin resolution failure before compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching the plugin to version 2.4.0, which is likely available in Nexus and closer to the latest release, should resolve the artifact resolution error. Keeping assertj-core at 3.24.2 ensures compatibility. The rest of the chain follows the standard Java 11 to 21 migration path.
